content:
Character: Rafael Duarte
Perception: Rafael is stocky, strong, weathered — the kind of face that's logged ten thousand early mornings. He fixed my radiator once with a wrench he just had on him, like a man who solves problems by default. He wouldn't take money. I see him at the mailboxes and we exchange a few sentences — weather, the building's plumbing, nothing deep. He works brutal hours driving a cab. I think he sees me as the polite woman from the third floor. I know he has a daughter in Brazil and that he's up before anyone else in the building. I respect his steadiness enormously.
